chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD. R.A.No. 102 of 2025 Gul Muhammad Vs. Imtiaz Ahmed 1. For orders on office objection No.7. 2. For hearing of CMA 1587/2025. 3. For hearing of main case. Date of hearing: 14.10.2025. Date of Order: 14.10.2025. Mr. Keval Kumar, Advocate for applicant. O R D E R JAWAD AKBAR SARWANA, J: Applicant/plaintiff has filed this Revision against the order dated 31.07.2025 passed by the learned District Judge, Badin, dismissing the applicant/plaintiff’s application for transfer of FC Suit No.102/2025 from the current trial Court to another. Counsel’s submission primarily is that the applicant/plaintiff’s grievance was triggered when Counsel’s adjournment application was allowed. Yet, the trial Court went ahead with the recording of the examination in chief of the Defendant in the absence of the applicant/plaintiff's counsel. On query, counsel submitted that the matter is at the stage of recording evidence. The bench also specifically queried the counsel to identify if, during the examination in chief, in his absence, any document had been brought on record that could not have been included in the recording of evidence. However, Counsel requested the bench that he would like to retain his right to raise such a challenge before the trial Court. He also conceded that although the trial Court has completed the examination in chief, as per the diary sheet, the trial Court has not closed the side of the applicant for cross-examining the witness, as yet. Given the above, there is nothing available on record, and none has been submitted during oral arguments in support of the revision by the applicant/plaintiff’s Counsel.

He also conceded that although the trial Court has completed the examination in chief, as per the diary sheet, the trial Court has not closed the side of the applicant for cross-examining the witness, as yet. Given the above, there is nothing available on record, and none has been submitted during oral arguments in support of the revision by the applicant/plaintiff’s Counsel. Accordingly, I do not find any reason in the instant case for transfer of the suit to a different trial Judge. Further, there is no defect in the impugned Order passed by the District Judge. Counsel for the applicant has argued the matter with verve, and it is expected that his conduct before the trial Court will remain professional and courteous. The Civil Revision Application, along with the listed application, is dismissed in the above terms. JUDGE Tufail
